sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.104 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group of order$p^a q^b \,$, where$p, q,$are distinct primes. Introduce distinct variables$x_p, x_q,$corresponding to$p, q,$~. Define functions$f,$from the lattice of subgroups of$G$to the polynomial ring${ Z}[x_p,x_q, ]$as follows: 0.5ex (1) if$H$has order$p^{ } q^{ } \,$, then$f(H)=x_p^{ } x_q^{ } \,$; 0.5ex (2) for all$H G$, we have$_{K H} (K)=f(H)$. 0.5ex Then$f(G), (G)$may be...
